post.description:
# For successful login, returns:
* 200 with MFA disabled
* 201 with MFA enabled when additional authentication factor is required

201.description: User authenticated, but an additional authentication factor is required

get.description:
Retrieves all devices

# Returns
- List of `Device` objects

- `WebError` if error occurs

403.description: You don't have permission to list all devices.

get.description:
Retrieve all devices that belong to specific `username`.

This endpoint requires `admin` role.

# Returns
- List of `Device` objects

- `WebError` object if error occurs.

403.description: You don't have permission to list user devices.

get.description:
Retrieve information about device based on their `device_id`

# Returns
- `Device` object

- `WebError` if error occurs

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: ID of device to update details.

400.description: Bad request, no networks found or device with pubkey that you want to send with is a server's pubkey.

example.msg: device's pubkey must be different from server's pubkey

404.description: Device not found.

put.description:
Update a device for a user by sending `ModifyDevice` object.

Notice that `wireguard_pubkey` must be different from server's pubkey.

Endpoint will trigger new update in gateway server.

# Returns
- `Device` object

- `WebError` if error occurs

400.description: Bad request, no networks found or device with pubkey that you want to send with is a server's pubkey.

example.msg: device's pubkey must be different from server's pubkey

404.description: Device not found.

post.description:
Add a new device for a user by sending `AddDevice` object.

Notice that `wireguard_pubkey` must be unique to successfully add the device.

You can't add devices for `disabled` users, unless you are an admin.

Device will be added to all networks in your company infrastructure.

User will receive all new device details on email.

# Returns
- `AddDeviceResult` object

- `WebError` if error occurs

201.description: Successfully added a new device for a user.

400.description: Bad request, no networks found or device with pubkey that you want to send with already exists.

401.description: Unauthorized to add a new device for a user.

403.description: You don't have permission to add a new device for a user. You can't add a new device for a disabled user.

500.description: Cannot add a new device for a user.

delete.description:
Delete user device and trigger new update in gateway server.

# Returns
- empty JSON

- `WebError` if error occurs

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: ID of device to update details.

404.description: Device not found.

get.summary: Retrieve paginated list of group names.
get.description:
Returns only the **names** of all groups as a paginated list.
Use `/api/v1/group-info` if you need full group details including ID, members, and VPN locations.

# Returns
- paginated list of group name strings

- `WebError` if error occurs

403.description: You don't have permission to retrieve groups.

post.description:
Create group based on `EditGroupInfo` object.

You can also choose whether group should grant admin privileges by changing `is_admin` parameter.

# Returns
- `EditGroupInfo` object

- `WebError` if error occurs

201.description: Successfully created a group and added users.

403.description: You don't have permission to create a group.

404.description: Cannot create group: user does not exist.

get.description:
For each group, the endpoint retrieves a `GroupInfo` object.

# Returns
- list of `GroupInfo` objects

- `WebError` if error occurs

403.description: You don't have permission to list groups info.

get.description:
Retrieves a `GroupInfo` object for the group with the given ID.

# Returns
- `GroupInfo` object

- `WebError` if error occurs

404.description: Incorrect ID of the group.

put.description:
Rename group and change members basing on `EditGroupInfo` object.

 You can also change `is_admin` parameter if you want to grant admin privileges to group members.

# Returns
- empty JSON

- `WebError` if error occurs

403.description: You don't have permission to update user group.

404.description: Cannot update group: user or group don't exist.

post.description:
Find a group by `id` and add `username` as a member.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

200.description: Successfully add a new member to group.

401.description: Unauthorized to add a new group member.

403.description: You don't have permission to add a new group member.

404.description: Cannot add a new group member: user or group don't exist.

500.description: Cannot add a new group member.

delete.description:
Delete group and remove all group members.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

403.description: You don't have permission to delete a group.

404.description: Cannot delete group: group not found.

delete.description:
Find a group by `id` and remove `username` as a member.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the group from which you want to remove a user.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Name of the user that you want to delete.

200.description: Successfully remove a member from group.

401.description: Unauthorized to remove a group member.

403.description: You don't have permission to remove a group member.

404.description: Cannot remove a group member: user or group don't exist.

post.description:
Assign many users to many groups at once basing on `BulkAssignToGroupsRequest` object.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

400.description: Bad request. Request contains users or groups that don't exist in db.

example.msg: Request contained users that doesn't exists in db.

401.description: Unauthorized to assign users to groups.

403.description: You don't have permission to assign users to groups.

example.msg: License signature doesn't match its content

404.description: License not found.

get.description:
# Returns
- Array of all OpenID providers and HTTP status "OK" on success.

# Returns
- List of `UserInfo` objects.

- `WebError` if error occurs

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Filter users by group names (OR logic - returns users in any of the specified groups)

parameters.name: no_group
parameters.description: Filter users with no group memberships. When combined with groups, returns the union (users in specified groups OR users with no groups).

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search across username, first name, last name, and email

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort key: name (default), username, or email

403.description: You don't have permission to list all users.

post.description:
Add a new user based on `AddUserData` object.

# Returns
- `UserInfo` object
- `WebError` if error occurs

403.description: You don't have permission to create a user.

post.summary: Verify if the user is available
post.description:
Check if user is available by provided `Username` object.
Username is unique so database returns only single user or nothing.

# Returns
- `200` if the user is available

- `WebError` if error occurs

**Please take notice that if user exists in database, endpoint will return status code 400.**

200.description: Provided username is available to use.

400.description: Bad request, provided username is not available or username is invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized to check is username available.

403.description: You don't have permission to check is username available.

500.description: Unable to check is username available.

put.description:
Changes your own password basing on `PasswordChangeSelf` object.

It can return error if password is not strong enough.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

400.description: Bad request, provided passwords are not same or new password does not satisfy requirements.

get.description:
Return a user based on provided username parameter.

# Returns
- `UserDetails` object
- `WebError` if error occurs

401.description: Unauthorized to return details about user.

403.description: You don't have permission to return details about user.

put.description:
Update user's data basing on `UserInfo` object, it can also remove/add authorized apps and groups assigned to user.

Endpoint is able to disable a user, but **admin cannot disable himself**.

Disabling a user can be done by setting `is_active` to `false`.



# Returns
- empty JSON

- `WebError` if error occurs

400.description: Bad request, unable to change user data. Verify user data that you want to update.

delete.description:
Deletes user, however, **you can't delete yourself as an administrator**.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

400.description: Bad request, unable to delete user.

403.description: You don't have permission to delete user.

404.description: User does not exist with username: <username>

delete.description:
Deletes an authorized application by `OAuth2` ID.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

403.description: You don't have permission to delete authorized app.

404.description: Incorrect authorized app, not found.

put.description:
Change user password basing on `PasswordChange` object, it can return error if password is not strong enough.

This endpoint doesn't allow you to **change your own** password.

If you want to change your own password please go to: `/api/v1/user/change_password`.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

400.description: Bad request, password does not satisfy requirements. This endpoint does not change your own password.

403.description: You don't have permission to change user password.

404.description: Cannot change user password that does not exist.

post.description:
Reset user password, it will send a new enrollment token to the user's email.

**This endpoint doesn't allow you to reset your own password.**

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

400.description: Bad request, this endpoint does not change your own password.

403.description: You don't have permission to change user password.

404.description: Cannot reset user password that does not exist.

500.description: Unable to send reset password to email

delete.description:
Delete WebAuthn security key that allows users to authenticate.

# Returns
- `WebError` if error occurs

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of security key that could point to passkey

403.description: You don't have permission to delete security key.

404.description: Incorrect authorized app, not found.

post.description:
Allows admin to start new remote desktop configuration for user that is provided as a parameter in endpoint.

Thanks to this endpoint you are able to receive a new desktop client configuration or update an existing one. Users need the configuration to connect to the company infrastrcture.

`Enrollment token` allows to start the process of gaining access to the company infrastructure **(The enrollment token is valid for 24 hours)**.

On the other hand, enrollment url allows the user to access the enrollment form via the web browser or perform the enrollment through the desktop client.

Optionally this endpoint can send an email notification to the user about the enrollment.

# Returns
- JSON with `enrollment_token` and `enrollment_url`

- `WebError` if error occurs

example.msg: Email notification is enabled, but email was not provided

401.description: Unauthorized to start remote desktop configuration.

example.msg: Can't create desktop configuration enrollment token for disabled user <username>

404.description: Provided user does not exist.

500.description: Unable to start remote desktop configuration.

post.description:
Allows admin to start new enrollment for user that is provided as a parameter in endpoint.

Thanks to this endpoint you are able to trigger manually enrollment process, where after finishing you receive an enrollment token.

**Enrollment token** allows to start the process of gaining access to the company infrastructure **(The enrollment token is valid for 24 hours)**.

On the other hand, enrollment url allows the user to access the enrollment form via the web browser or perform the enrollment through the desktop client.

Optionally this endpoint can send an email notification to the user about the enrollment.

# Returns
- JSON with `enrollment_token` and `enrollment_url`

- `WebError` if error occurs

example.msg: Email notification is enabled, but email was not provided

403.description: You don't have permission to start enrollment.

404.description: Provided user does not exist.

AliasKind.description:
ACL alias can be of one of the following types:
- Destination: the alias defines a complete destination that an ACL rule applies to
- Component: the alias defines parts of a destination and will be combined with other parts
  manually defined in an ACL rule

AliasState.description:
ACL alias can be in one of the following states:
- Applied: the alias can be used in ACL rules
- Modified: the alias has been modified and the changes have not yet been applied

Unlike ACL rules themselves aliases do not require a `New` state,
since they do not cause any changes to locations until they
are used by a rule.
`Deleted` state is also omitted since we don't allow deleting if an alias is used by any rules.

ApiAclAlias.description:
API representation of [`AclAlias`] for "Alias Component" (not "Destination").
All relations represented as arrays of IDs.

ApiAclDestination.description:
API representation of [`AclAlias`] for "Destination" (not "Alias Component").
All relations represented as arrays of IDs.

configured.description:
Whether the device should be considered as setup and ready to use
or does it require some additional steps to be taken. Not configured devices
won't be sent to the gateway. It is assumed that an unconfigured device is already
added to all networks it should be in, but it's not ready to be used yet due to
e.g. public key not properly set up yet.

Whether the device should be considered as setup and ready to use
or does it require some additional steps to be taken. Not configured devices
won't be sent to the gateway. It is assumed that an unconfigured device is already
added to all networks it should be in, but it's not ready to be used yet due to
e.g. public key not properly set up yet.

EditAclAlias.description: API representation of [`AclAlias`] used in API requests for modification operations.

EditAclDestination.description: API representation of [`AclAlias`] used in API requests for modification operations

EditAclRule.description: API representation of [`AclRule`] used in API requests for modification operations

EditGroupInfo.description: Dedicated `GroupInfo` variant for group modification operations.

public_ip.description: New public IP address for SNAT

user_id.description: User ID to bind to the public IP

WebError.description: Represents kinds of error that occurred

WireguardNetwork.description: Stores configuration required to setup a WireGuard network

WireguardNetwork_i64.description: Stores configuration required to setup a WireGuard network

tags.name: user
tags.description:
### Endpoints for managing users
Available actions:
- list all users
- disable/enable user
- CRUD mechanism for handling users
- operations on security key and authorized app
- change user password.
- start remote desktop configuratiion
- trigger enrollment process

tags.name: group
tags.description:
### Endpoints for managing groups
Available actions:
- list all groups
- CRUD mechanism for handling groups
- add or delete a group member
- remove group
- bulk assign users to groups

tags.name: device
tags.description:
### Endpoints for managing devices

Available actions:
- list all devices or user devices
- CRUD mechanism for handling devices.

tags.name: network
tags.description:
### Endpoints that allow to control your networks.

Available actions:
- list all wireguard networks
- CRUD mechanism for handling devices.

tags.name: SNAT
tags.description:
### Endpoints that allow you to control user SNAT bindings for your locations.

Available actions:
- list all SNAT bindings
- create new SNAT binding
- modify SNAT binding
- delete SNAT binding
